commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Contributor proof and maintainer security review are required before this PR can become a landing candidate.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally relaxes an SSRF guard and should be narrowed to the same host-scoped fake-IP boundary used by existing provider fetches.; Review finding: [P2] Scope the fake-IP exemption to OpenAI hosts — extensions/openai/realtime-provider-shared.ts:109 (sha=e3b9c11cd3b2e2491044729675a69d8b2510e628)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an RFC2544 fake-IP SSRF policy opt-in to the OpenAI Realtime client-secret fetch and asserts that policy in two voice-provider tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +2. Total +3 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main calls fetchWithSsrFGuard for Realtime client secrets without an RFC2544 policy, while the SSRF guard blocks 198.18.0.0/15 unless that policy is set. I did not run a live Clash or Surge setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor proof and maintainer security review are required before this PR can become a landing candidate.

target.title: fix(openai): allow RFC 2544 fake-IP range for Realtime session requests
target.body:
## Summary

- `fetchWithSsrFGuard` in `createOpenAIRealtimeSecret` was missing `policy: { allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange: true }`, causing the SSRF guard to block requests when proxy stacks (Clash, Surge, sing-box in TUN/fake-IP mode) resolve `api.openai.com` to the `198.18.0.0/15` special-use range.
- The same opt-in already exists on other provider HTTP call paths; this fix closes the gap for the Realtime session-secret fetch path.
- Added `policy` field assertions to both existing fetch-request checks in `realtime-voice-provider.test.ts` to prevent silent regression.

Closes part of #56535.

## Verification

```
pnpm test extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts
```

- Test Files: 1 passed
- Tests: 48 passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: SSRF guard blocks OpenAI Realtime session-secret requests when a TUN/fake-IP proxy resolves `api.openai.com` → `198.18.0.0/15`
Real environment tested: macOS, Clash TUN/fake-IP mode
Exact steps or command run after this patch: start gateway with Clash TUN active; initiate a Realtime voice session
Evidence after fix: session-secret fetch succeeds; WebSocket connects normally
Observed result after fix: no SSRF block error in gateway logs
What was not tested: live Surge / sing-box environments (same code path)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: shushushv
